name: GLM-5
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), 200K context, trained on a 100k-GPU Huawei Ascend 910B cluster (no Nvidia/AMD/Intel silicon)
note: Major-version flagship succeeding GLM-4.6; first Z.ai model released after Zhipu's Jan 2026 Hong Kong IPO; widely reported as trained entirely on domestic Huawei chips, no Nvidia used.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; Z.ai's own claimed 'leading open-model score' at launch
Terminal-Bench 2.0 (Terminus-2 harness); alt config reaches 60.7 per HF model card
Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools; with-tools variant reaches 50.4
BrowseComp, no context-management; with context-management reaches 75.9
tau2-Bench, labeled 'T2-Bench' on HF model card

IFBench (instruction-following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis, one of the Intelligence Evaluations shown on GLM-5 (Reasoning)'s live AA model page (72%, ranked 13th of 23 models shown). Not reported by Z.ai in its own launch materials (which cover HLE/AIME/GPQA/SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench/CyberGym/BrowseComp/tau2-bench, all already tracked) and not previously present in the corpus for GLM-5.

verificationNotes: This is a genuine, released model, not a rumor -- confirmed via zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub repo, Hugging Face model card, official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5, arXiv technical report 2602.15763 ('GLM-5: from Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering'), and extensive independent tech press (Let's Data Science, Pandaily-style outlets, Wikipedia's GLM(AI) article) all covering the Feb 2026 launch and its 'trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips, zero Nvidia' angle, tied to Zhipu's Jan 8 2026 HKEX IPO. Release date: most sources say Feb 11, 2026; docs.z.ai's own release-notes timeline says Feb 12 -- one-day discrepancy, used the more commonly-cited Feb 11. Params: GitHub repo table and multiple press pieces agree on 744B total / 40B active; a couple of secondary blogs round to '745B/44B' -- treated as noise around the same figure, GitHub repo (self-published) used as primary. Benchmark scores for SWE-bench Verified (77.8) and Terminal-Bench 2.0 (56.2) confirmed directly on the official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5 page. HLE, AIME, GPQA-Diamond, CyberGym, BrowseComp, and tau2-Bench scores confirmed directly on the official Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-5), which lists a full self-reported benchmark table. Omitted SWE-bench Multilingual (73.3), BrowseComp-Zh (72.7), MCP-Atlas (67.8), Tool-Decathlon (38.0), and Vending Bench 2 ($4,432 balance) because they either lack a matching corpus slug or would require misfiling a distinct benchmark variant under an existing base slug.
